task_completion.description: Vital Recorder application is open with Case #6 loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible. The title bar, file explorer pane, or track list indicates that Case 6 (e.g., '6.vital' or '0006.vital') is currently loaded.

task_completion.description: Vital sign waveforms are displayed in the main viewing area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main graph area of Vital Recorder contains visible data tracks (e.g., waveforms or numeric trends for vital signs), rather than being blank or empty.

task_completion.description: The timeline is centered at the 20-minute mark (00:20:00).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The time axis (x-axis) of the waveform display is centered around 20 minutes (00:20:00 or 1200 seconds). The middle of the screen should correspond to this timestamp.

task_completion.description: The timeline zoom level shows a 5-minute window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible span of the time axis (from the left edge to the right edge of the graph) covers exactly or approximately 5 minutes (e.g., spanning from 00:17:30 to 00:22:30).

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the view is saved to the specified Desktop path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'case_review_capture.png' is visible on the Desktop, or a file explorer window shows this file located in 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Vital Recorder GUI to adjust the view.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Vital Recorder interface open with the timeline and zoom controls adjusted, rather than just terminal commands executing a script.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot is a genuine capture of the Vital Recorder application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used a screenshot tool or built-in OS feature to capture the screen, rather than generating a fake image using a Python script (e.g., matplotlib) or downloading an external image.

integrity.description: The agent loaded an actual .vital file into the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application shows genuine complex waveform data typical of a surgical case, not a mock file or manually drawn lines.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Case 6 is a valid VitalDB case with a total duration of 85.75 minutes. The target time of 00:20:00 (1200 seconds) is roughly 23.3% into the case. A 5-minute window centered at 20 minutes means the x-axis should span approximately from 00:17:30 to 00:22:30. Look for evidence of file '0006.vital' or '6.vital' loaded in the Vital Recorder UI.
